title: Chunked Transfer Coding, Extensions, Trailers, and Decoding · Failure Repair Bay
summary: An incident engineer receives a plausible but defective incremental chunked decoder and trailer policy; retain the raw transcript, isolate the first divergent state, and preserve a regression corpus entry. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in incremental chunked decoder and trailer policy and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Parse bounded hexadecimal chunk size with extensions, overflow checks, exact CRLF, and cumulative decoded limits.
Read exactly each chunk-data length plus its terminating CRLF across arbitrary fragmentation.
Treat the last zero-sized chunk as the transition to trailer parsing rather than immediate message completion.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Order the regression proof · Debug Chunked Changed Corpus
steps.instruction:
Order the Chunked Changed Corpus evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Order the regression proof · Debug Chunked Changed Corpus.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Chunked Changed Corpus assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Order the regression proof · Debug Chunked Changed Corpus, keep Chunked Changed Corpus connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

content.text: A debug protocol team handles case 9193 in a protocol upgrade gateway transferring buffered tunnel bytes. Build deterministic Go evidence to bound chunk accumulation and require trailer validation after the zero chunk; preserve this operating constraint: the browser model cannot open a socket or claim packet-level proof; then lower one byte or time budget and prove rejection occurs before allocation or dispatch. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Test extensions, leading zeros, split size lines, missing CRLF, overflow, excess trailer bytes, forbidden fields, and a pipelined next message.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Chunked Changed Corpus has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Test extensions, leading zeros, split size lines, missing CRLF, overflow, excess trailer bytes, forbidden fields, and a pipelined next message.

hints: For Publish the incident record · Debug Chunked Changed Corpus, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Chunked Changed Corpus precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Publish the incident record · Debug Chunked Changed Corpus, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A debug protocol team handles case 9201 in a canary edge service advertising HTTP/3 with bounded fallback. Build deterministic Go evidence to bound chunk accumulation and require trailer validation after the zero chunk; preserve this operating constraint: the browser model cannot open a socket or claim packet-level proof; then append a second pipelined message and prove the first parser leaves the exact residual bytes. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Test extensions, leading zeros, split size lines, missing CRLF, overflow, excess trailer bytes, forbidden fields, and a pipelined next message.

For Publish the incident record · Debug Chunked Changed Corpus, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: One multi-chunk happy path proves a chunked decoder preserves every message boundary.
